items.title: Documenting Persecution
items.description: We meticulously track, verify, and report every incident of Christian persecution across India, ensuring no act goes unnoticed.

items.title: Legal & Emergency Aid
items.description: Providing immediate legal assistance, shelter, and financial relief to victims and their families in times of crisis.

items.title: Advocacy & Awareness
items.description: Engaging with national and international bodies to raise awareness and pressure for religious freedom and protection.

sections.title: Our Mission: Defending Faith, Delivering Hope

items.title: 24/7 Helpline Support
items.description: Our dedicated toll-free helpline offers immediate assistance, guidance, and emotional support to those facing persecution. We are always there for them.

items.title: Comprehensive Documentation
items.description: We compile detailed reports and statistics on anti-Christian incidents, providing irrefutable evidence for advocacy and action. This data is critical for change.

items.title: Financial Relief Programs
items.description: Direct financial aid supports displaced families, medical emergencies, and livelihood restoration for victims of faith-based violence. Your donations directly help.

items.title: Robust Legal Defense
items.description: Our network of lawyers offers pro-bono legal counsel and representation to unjustly accused or imprisoned Christians, ensuring their rights are protected. We fight for justice.

items.title: Global Prayer Networks
items.description: We mobilize a global community of prayer warriors to intercede for the persecuted, believing in the power of collective prayer for spiritual strength. Join us in prayer.

items.title: International Advocacy
items.description: We amplify the voices of the voiceless on international platforms, urging governments and human rights organizations to address religious freedom violations. We speak for them.

items.title: Comprehensive Reporting
items.description: We provide accurate, verifiable data on persecution, forming the bedrock for effective intervention and policy change.

items.title: Holistic Support
items.description: From immediate physical needs to long-term legal and emotional support, we address every facet of a victim's suffering.

items.title: Global Reach, Local Impact
items.description: While our focus is India, our advocacy contributes to the global fight for religious freedom, making a difference where it matters most.

items.title: Empowering Communities
items.description: We empower local communities with resources and knowledge to endure persecution and stand firm in their faith.

items.title: Unyielding Advocacy
items.description: We tirelessly champion the rights of the persecuted, ensuring their stories are heard and justice is pursued.

items.title: Transparent & Trustworthy
items.description: Our commitment to integrity means your contributions directly impact those in need, with full transparency in our operations.

sections.title: Why Persecution Relief is Essential
sections.subtitle: Critical support for a growing global crisis.

sections.body: Every donation, every prayer, every act of advocacy brings relief to a persecuted soul. Stand with us against injustice. Your action today can change a life tomorrow.
sections.title: Your Generosity Fuels Hope. Join Our Cause Today!

items.quote: When our village church was destroyed, Persecution Relief provided immediate shelter and legal aid. They literally saved our community from despair. We're forever grateful for their unwavering support.
items.author: Pastor John M.

items.quote: My husband was wrongly imprisoned, and we felt utterly alone. Persecution Relief’s legal team fought tirelessly for his release and supported our family through the ordeal. Their dedication is truly inspiring.
items.author: Priya S.

items.quote: The financial assistance from Persecution Relief allowed me to rebuild my small business after it was vandalized for my faith. They don't just advocate; they provide tangible means of recovery.
items.author: Ravi K.

sections.title: Who We Are

settings.customHtml:
Who We Are

Persecution Relief is a registered nonprofit organization dedicated to reporting, documenting, and responding to religious persecution against Christians in India. Founded on the principle that every believer has the right to practice their faith without fear, we operate a 24/7 toll-free helpline (1800-1234-461) and coordinate rapid response teams across the country.

We work with local churches, legal professionals, medical teams, and government agencies to provide comprehensive support to victims and their families.

sections.customHtml:
Who We Are

Persecution Relief is a registered nonprofit organization dedicated to reporting, documenting, and responding to religious persecution against Christians in India. Founded on the principle that every believer has the right to practice their faith without fear, we operate a 24/7 toll-free helpline (1800-1234-461) and coordinate rapid response teams across the country.

We work with local churches, legal professionals, medical teams, and government agencies to provide comprehensive support to victims and their families.

sections.title: Our Founder

settings.customHtml:
Our Founder

Persecution Relief was founded by a passionate advocate for religious freedom who witnessed firsthand the suffering of persecuted believers in India. Driven by faith and a commitment to justice, the organization was established to be a voice for the voiceless and a shield for the vulnerable.

Under visionary leadership, Persecution Relief has grown from a small prayer group into a nationwide network of volunteers, lawyers, and advocates serving thousands of persecuted Christians every year.

sections.customHtml:
Our Founder

Persecution Relief was founded by a passionate advocate for religious freedom who witnessed firsthand the suffering of persecuted believers in India. Driven by faith and a commitment to justice, the organization was established to be a voice for the voiceless and a shield for the vulnerable.

Under visionary leadership, Persecution Relief has grown from a small prayer group into a nationwide network of volunteers, lawyers, and advocates serving thousands of persecuted Christians every year.

sections.title: Our Goal

settings.customHtml:
Our Goal

To create a nation where every individual can freely worship and practice their Christian faith without fear of violence, intimidation, social boycott, or legal harassment. We envision an India where religious liberty is not just a constitutional promise but a lived reality for every believer.

sections.customHtml:
Our Goal

To create a nation where every individual can freely worship and practice their Christian faith without fear of violence, intimidation, social boycott, or legal harassment. We envision an India where religious liberty is not just a constitutional promise but a lived reality for every believer.

sections.title: Our Mission

settings.customHtml:
Our Mission

To document, report, and respond to Christian persecution in India through four pillars of service: Prayer, Financial Aid, Political Advocacy, and Judicial Support. We stand with the persecuted church, providing immediate relief and long-term rehabilitation to victims and their families.

sections.customHtml:
Our Mission

sections.title: Our Motto

settings.customHtml:
Our Motto

"Serving Persecuted, Loving Persecutors"

This motto encapsulates our belief that the Christian response to persecution must be rooted in love — love for those who suffer, and even love for those who cause the suffering. We seek justice while extending grace.

sections.customHtml:
Our Motto

This motto encapsulates our belief that the Christian response to persecution must be rooted in love — love for those who suffer, and even love for those who cause the suffering. We seek justice while extending grace.

sections.title: Our Vision

settings.customHtml:
Our Vision

To be the leading voice for persecuted Christians in India, building a comprehensive network of prayer, advocacy, legal aid, and community support that ensures no believer faces persecution alone. We envision a movement that transforms communities through compassion, justice, and the power of united prayer.

sections.customHtml:
Our Vision

We envision a movement that transforms communities through compassion, justice, and the power of united prayer.

sections.title: Our Statement of Faith

settings.customHtml:
Our Statement of Faith

We believe in one God, eternally existing in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. We believe in the divine inspiration and authority of the Holy Scriptures. We believe in the deity of our Lord Jesus Christ, His virgin birth, His sinless life, His miracles, His atoning death, His bodily resurrection, and His personal return in power and glory.

We believe that salvation is by grace through faith in Jesus Christ alone. We believe in the present ministry of the Holy Spirit by whose indwelling the Christian is enabled to live a godly life. We believe in the spiritual unity of believers in our Lord Jesus Christ.

sections.title: Persecution Promised

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Promised

"Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution." — 2 Timothy 3:12

The Bible makes clear that persecution is not an anomaly but a promise. Jesus warned His followers that they would face opposition, hatred, and suffering for His name. Throughout Scripture, we see this pattern repeated — from the prophets of the Old Testament to the apostles of the New Testament. Persecution is woven into the fabric of faithful discipleship. Understanding this biblical truth helps believers prepare for and endure trials with steadfast faith.

sections.customHtml:

Understanding this biblical truth helps believers prepare for and endure trials with steadfast faith.

sections.title: Persecution of Jesus

settings.customHtml:
Persecution of Jesus

"If the world hates you, keep in mind that it hated me first." — John 15:18

Jesus Christ, the Son of God, faced the ultimate persecution. From His birth when Herod sought to kill Him, through His ministry when religious leaders plotted against Him, to His crucifixion on the cross — Jesus endured every form of persecution. His suffering was not in vain but accomplished the salvation of humanity. As followers of Christ, we walk in His footsteps, knowing that our Lord understands our suffering intimately.

sections.title: Persecution Birth

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Birth

"A time is coming when anyone who kills you will think they are offering a service to God." — John 16:2

The birth of the Christian church was accompanied by intense persecution. From the stoning of Stephen, the first Christian martyr, to the scattering of believers from Jerusalem, persecution served as the catalyst for the spread of the Gospel. The early church grew not despite persecution but through it, as believers carried their faith to new regions and peoples. This pattern continues today as the persecuted church remains one of the fastest-growing segments of Christianity worldwide.

sections.title: Persecution Types

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Types

"Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven." — Matthew 5:10

Christian persecution takes many forms: physical violence, destruction of property, social ostracism, economic boycott, false legal charges, forced conversion, denial of education and employment, and psychological intimidation. In India, believers face mob attacks on churches, assault of pastors, burning of Bibles, and harassment under anti-conversion laws. Understanding the various types of persecution helps us respond effectively and provide appropriate support to victims.

sections.customHtml:

sections.title: Persecution Reaction

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Reaction

"But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you." — Matthew 5:44

The Christian response to persecution is unique among all faiths. Rather than retaliation or revenge, believers are called to respond with love, prayer, and forgiveness. This does not mean passivity — we actively seek justice through legal means, advocate for policy changes, and provide protection to the vulnerable. But our ultimate response is grounded in the love of Christ, who forgave His persecutors from the cross.

sections.customHtml:

sections.title: Persecution Perspective

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Perspective

"For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all." — 2 Corinthians 4:17

An eternal perspective transforms how we view persecution. While suffering is real and painful, Scripture teaches that it produces perseverance, character, and hope. The persecuted church throughout history has demonstrated remarkable faith, joy, and spiritual vitality precisely because they have learned to view their circumstances through the lens of eternity. This perspective does not minimize suffering but places it within God's greater purpose.

sections.customHtml:

sections.title: Persecution Fear

settings.customHtml:
Persecution Fear

"Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul." — Matthew 10:28

Fear is a natural response to persecution, but Scripture repeatedly calls believers to courage. Many Christians in India live in daily fear — fear of mob violence, fear of false charges, fear of losing their livelihood. Persecution Relief works to address both the spiritual and practical dimensions of fear, providing safety, legal protection, community support, and spiritual encouragement to help believers stand firm in their faith despite threats and intimidation.

sections.title: Persecution of Paul

settings.customHtml:
Persecution of Paul

"I have been in danger from rivers, in danger from bandits, in danger from my fellow Jews, in danger from Gentiles." — 2 Corinthians 11:26

The Apostle Paul, once a persecutor of Christians himself, became one of the most persecuted believers in history. He was beaten, stoned, shipwrecked, imprisoned, and eventually martyred for his faith. Yet Paul considered all his suffering as nothing compared to knowing Christ. His letters from prison became foundational texts of the Christian faith, and his example continues to inspire persecuted believers around the world to endure with joy and purpose.

sections.title: Persecution India

settings.customHtml:
Persecution India

"Remember those in prison as if you were together with them in prison, and those who are mistreated as if you yourselves were suffering." — Hebrews 13:3

India is home to over 30 million Christians who face increasing levels of persecution. Incidents of violence against churches, pastors, and believers have risen dramatically in recent years. Anti-conversion laws in several states are used to harass and imprison Christians. Social boycotts, forced reconversions, and mob violence are common in rural areas. Persecution Relief documents hundreds of incidents each year and works tirelessly to provide support, legal aid, and advocacy for the persecuted church in India.

sections.customHtml:

sections.title: Deaths of Disciples

settings.customHtml:
Deaths of Disciples

"Be faithful, even to the point of death, and I will give you life as your victor's crown." — Revelation 2:10

The twelve disciples of Jesus, with the exception of John, all died as martyrs for their faith. Peter was crucified upside down, James was beheaded, Andrew was crucified on an X-shaped cross, and Thomas was speared to death in India. Their willingness to die for their testimony of the risen Christ stands as powerful evidence of their conviction and as an enduring example for persecuted believers throughout the ages.

pages.title: How to Pray for Persecuted Church

sections.title: How to Pray for the Persecuted Church

settings.customHtml:
How to Pray for the Persecuted Church

"Pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective." — James 5:16

Prayer is the most powerful weapon in supporting the persecuted church. Pray for protection and safety for believers facing violence. Pray for courage and faith for those enduring imprisonment. Pray for comfort and provision for families who have lost loved ones. Pray for justice in legal cases. Pray for the hearts of persecutors to be transformed. Pray for government leaders to uphold religious freedom. Pray for Persecution Relief and other organizations serving the persecuted. Your prayers make a real difference.

sections.title: Bless the Persecutors in Jesus Name

settings.customHtml:
Bless the Persecutors in Jesus Name

"Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse." — Romans 12:14

One of the most radical teachings of Jesus is to bless those who persecute us. This is not natural — it is supernatural. When we choose to bless rather than curse, we break the cycle of hatred and open the door for God's transformative power. Many former persecutors have become powerful evangelists after encountering the love and forgiveness of the believers they once tormented. At Persecution Relief, our motto — "Serving Persecuted, Loving Persecutors" — reflects this biblical command.

pages.title: I Am Jesus Whom You Are Persecuting

sections.title: I Am Jesus, Whom You Are Persecuting

settings.customHtml:
I Am Jesus, Whom You Are Persecuting

"He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, 'Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?' 'Who are you, Lord?' Saul asked. 'I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.'" — Acts 9:4-5

When Saul of Tarsus was confronted by the risen Christ on the road to Damascus, Jesus identified Himself with the persecuted believers. To persecute Christians is to persecute Christ Himself. This profound truth reveals that Jesus stands in solidarity with every believer who suffers for His name. It also serves as a solemn warning to those who persecute — they are not merely opposing people, they are opposing the Lord of all creation.

sections.title: Prayerfully

settings.customHtml:
Prayerfully

Prayer is the foundation of everything we do at Persecution Relief. We organize prayer chains, prayer vigils, and national days of prayer for persecuted Christians across India. Our prayer network connects thousands of intercessors who lift up specific requests from victims and their families. Join us in standing in the gap for those who suffer for their faith.

sections.title: Financially

settings.customHtml:
Financially

Persecution often leaves victims and their families in desperate financial need. Medical bills from violent attacks, legal fees for false charges, loss of livelihood from social boycotts — the financial impact of persecution is devastating. Persecution Relief provides emergency financial assistance, medical support, legal aid funding, and long-term rehabilitation support to help families rebuild their lives.

sections.customHtml:

Persecution Relief provides emergency financial assistance, medical support, legal aid funding, and long-term rehabilitation support to help families rebuild their lives.

sections.title: Politically

settings.customHtml:
Politically

Religious freedom is a fundamental right guaranteed by the Indian Constitution. Yet anti-conversion laws and political movements threaten the rights of Christians across India. Persecution Relief engages in political advocacy at the state and national level, working with legislators, filing legal challenges against unjust laws, and raising awareness about the political dimensions of religious persecution.

sections.customHtml:
Politically

Persecution Relief engages in political advocacy at the state and national level, working with legislators, filing legal challenges against unjust laws, and raising awareness about the political dimensions of religious persecution.

sections.title: Judicially

settings.customHtml:
Judicially

Many persecuted Christians face false criminal charges, illegal detention, and denial of due process. Persecution Relief provides legal representation through a network of Christian lawyers and advocates. We file FIRs (First Information Reports) on behalf of victims, challenge unjust arrests, and pursue justice in courts of law. Our judicial team has successfully secured the release of hundreds of wrongfully detained pastors and believers.

footer.description: Persecution Relief is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting persecuted Christians in India through aid, advocacy, and comprehensive documentation.

children.label: How to Pray for the Persecuted Church
Bless the Persecutors in Jesus Name
I Am Jesus, Whom You Are Persecuting

items.title: Documenting Persecution
items.description: We meticulously track, verify, and report every incident of Christian persecution across India, ensuring no act goes unnoticed.

items.title: Legal & Emergency Aid
items.description: Providing immediate legal assistance, shelter, and financial relief to victims and their families in times of crisis.

items.title: Advocacy & Awareness
items.description: Engaging with national and international bodies to raise awareness and pressure for religious freedom and protection.

sections.title: Our Mission: Defending Faith, Delivering Hope

items.title: 24/7 Helpline Support
items.description: Our dedicated toll-free helpline offers immediate assistance, guidance, and emotional support to those facing persecution. We are always there for them.

items.title: Comprehensive Documentation
items.description: We compile detailed reports and statistics on anti-Christian incidents, providing irrefutable evidence for advocacy and action. This data is critical for change.

items.title: Financial Relief Programs
items.description: Direct financial aid supports displaced families, medical emergencies, and livelihood restoration for victims of faith-based violence. Your donations directly help.

items.title: Robust Legal Defense
items.description: Our network of lawyers offers pro-bono legal counsel and representation to unjustly accused or imprisoned Christians, ensuring their rights are protected. We fight for justice.

items.title: Global Prayer Networks
items.description: We mobilize a global community of prayer warriors to intercede for the persecuted, believing in the power of collective prayer for spiritual strength. Join us in prayer.

items.title: International Advocacy
items.description: We amplify the voices of the voiceless on international platforms, urging governments and human rights organizations to address religious freedom violations. We speak for them.

items.title: Comprehensive Reporting
items.description: We provide accurate, verifiable data on persecution, forming the bedrock for effective intervention and policy change.

items.title: Holistic Support
items.description: From immediate physical needs to long-term legal and emotional support, we address every facet of a victim's suffering.

items.title: Global Reach, Local Impact
items.description: While our focus is India, our advocacy contributes to the global fight for religious freedom, making a difference where it matters most.

items.title: Empowering Communities
items.description: We empower local communities with resources and knowledge to endure persecution and stand firm in their faith.

items.title: Unyielding Advocacy
items.description: We tirelessly champion the rights of the persecuted, ensuring their stories are heard and justice is pursued.

items.title: Transparent & Trustworthy
items.description: Our commitment to integrity means your contributions directly impact those in need, with full transparency in our operations.

sections.title: Why Persecution Relief is Essential
sections.subtitle: Critical support for a growing global crisis.

sections.body: Every donation, every prayer, every act of advocacy brings relief to a persecuted soul. Stand with us against injustice. Your action today can change a life tomorrow.
sections.title: Your Generosity Fuels Hope. Join Our Cause Today!

items.quote: When our village church was destroyed, Persecution Relief provided immediate shelter and legal aid. They literally saved our community from despair. We're forever grateful for their unwavering support.
items.author: Pastor John M.

items.quote: My husband was wrongly imprisoned, and we felt utterly alone. Persecution Relief’s legal team fought tirelessly for his release and supported our family through the ordeal. Their dedication is truly inspiring.
items.author: Priya S.

items.quote: The financial assistance from Persecution Relief allowed me to rebuild my small business after it was vandalized for my faith. They don't just advocate; they provide tangible means of recovery.
items.author: Ravi K.
